title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
summary: Introduced Mamba, a selective state space model that achieves linear-time sequence modeling while matching or exceeding Transformer performance. By making the state space parameters input-dependent (selective), Mamba can perform content-based reasoning that previous SSMs could not. The hardware-aware implementation achieves 3-5x faster inference than Transformers of equivalent size.
key_contributions: Introduced selective state spaces — making SSM parameters input-dependent
Achieved linear-time scaling in sequence length vs. quadratic for attention
Hardware-aware parallel scan implementation for efficient GPU computation
Matched Transformer performance on language modeling while being significantly faster
Opened a credible alternative path to the dominant Transformer architecture
impact: Mamba represents the most credible challenge to Transformer dominance since the architecture was introduced. Its linear-time scaling could enable efficient processing of much longer sequences, and it sparked significant research interest in state space models as alternatives or complements to attention-based architectures.
query_hooks: What is selective state space modeling?
